description: A state machine whose apply step depends on local time, RNG, or map iteration order produces different state on each replica from the same log.
body: Failure mode: replicas agree on the ordered log but the apply function reads wall-clock time, a local random source, or iterates an unordered map, so identical inputs yield different committed state across nodes. Detection signals: replicas diverge at the same commit index despite identical logs; divergence is non-reproducible and correlates with timing; the apply path calls now(), rand(), or unordered iteration. Do not allow any non-deterministic input into the apply path. Safer fallback: make apply pure and deterministic by threading required time/randomness through the log entry itself, use ordered iteration, and add a cross-replica state-hash check at committed indices to catch divergence early.
